message:
fix(cli,server): bounded port-holder PID lookup via ss/lsof and targeted process cleanup

Replace unbounded fuser-based port killing with a structured PID
discovery pipeline that only terminates Agent Mail processes, reducing
blast radius when an unrelated service occupies the configured port.

Startup checks:
- Add agent_mail_port_holder_pids() as public API for filtered PID
  discovery (port → listener PIDs → filter by Agent Mail binary)
- Add port_holder_pids() with platform-aware fallback: ss on Linux
  (parses "pid=N" from socket state output), lsof elsewhere
- Parse Content-Length header for bounded health-check body reads
  instead of waiting for EOF on keep-alive connections
- Add MAX_HEALTH_BODY_BYTES (4KB) constant for read limits
- Add parse_content_length(), parse_ss_port_holder_pids(), and
  parse_lsof_port_holder_pids() with unit tests for each

CLI port cleanup:
- Rewrite kill_port_holder() to use agent_mail_port_holder_pids()
  instead of raw fuser/lsof invocations, sending SIGTERM then
  SIGKILL only to confirmed Agent Mail PIDs
- Check port status between SIGTERM and SIGKILL phases to skip
  force-kill when graceful shutdown succeeded
- Only sleep after SIGKILL if there were actually surviving processes

Code formatting:
- Apply rustfmt to s3fifo.rs ghost eviction, index_layout.rs
  symlink creation, config.rs envfile writes, storage.rs markdown
  processing, build_slots.rs lease writes, and main.rs imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
